item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-009","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":null,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":null,"over_forced_verdict":null,"rationale":"The answer rejects the reduction, identifies Kubernetes as a container application cluster operating/orchestration/control system, and explicitly separates scheduling as one capability over Pods rather than ordinary processes. It also explains the locally true layer: scheduling is a rough intuition only for workload placement, while the whole object is declarative lifecycle and desired-state control across a cluster. It does not use the forbidden '可以这么理解' style concession."}
